414	        mb_r = ScalarType.maybe_parse(value)
415	        if mb_r is None:
416	            raise AssertionError(f"unknown dtype {value}")
417	        return mb_r
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    def parse_set(values: str) -> OrderedSet[ScalarType]:
421	        dtypes: OrderedSet[ScalarType] = OrderedSet()
422	        for value in values.split(", "):
423	            if value in DTYPE_CLASSES:
424	                dtypes.update(DTYPE_CLASSES[value])
425	            else:
426	                dtypes.add(ScalarType.parse(value))
427	        return dtypes
428	
429	
430	DTYPE_CLASSES: dict[str, OrderedSet[ScalarType]] = {}
431	# NB: Integral doesn't include boolean
432	DTYPE_CLASSES["Integral"] = OrderedSet(
433	    [
434	        ScalarType.Byte,
435	        ScalarType.Char,
436	        ScalarType.Int,
437	        ScalarType.Long,
438	        ScalarType.Short,
439	    ]
440	)
441	# NB: Floating doesn't include low precision types
442	DTYPE_CLASSES["Floating"] = OrderedSet([ScalarType.Float, ScalarType.Double])
443	DTYPE_CLASSES["Complex"] = OrderedSet(
444	    [ScalarType.ComplexFloat, ScalarType.ComplexDouble]
445	)
446	DTYPE_CLASSES["All"] = DTYPE_CLASSES["Integral"] | DTYPE_CLASSES["Floating"]
447	DTYPE_CLASSES["AllAndComplex"] = DTYPE_CLASSES["All"] | DTYPE_CLASSES["Complex"]
448	DTYPE_CLASSES["FloatingAndComplex"] = (
449	    DTYPE_CLASSES["Floating"] | DTYPE_CLASSES["Complex"]
450	)
451	
452	
453	# Represents the valid entries for ufunc_inner_loop in native_functions.yaml.
454	# NB: if you add a new UfuncKey, you will teach torchgen.dest.ufunc how
455	# to process it.  Most logic will ignore keys they don't understand, so your
456	# new key will get silently ignored until you hook in logic to deal with it.
457	class UfuncKey(Enum):
458	    # These are low level keys that represent exactly one particular
459	    # instantiation of the kernel produced by codegen
460	    CUDAFunctor = auto()
461	    CUDAFunctorOnOther = auto()
462	    CUDAFunctorOnSelf = auto()
463	
464	    CPUScalar = auto()
465	    CPUVector = auto()
466	
467	    # These are the ones users will usually specify, and
468	    # implicitly "fill in" the low level keys
469	    ScalarOnly = auto()  # CUDA*, CPUScalar
470	    Generic = auto()  # CUDA*, CPU*
471	
472	    def __str__(self) -> str:
473	        return self.name
474	
475	    @staticmethod
476	    def parse(value: str) -> UfuncKey:
477	        for k, v in UfuncKey.__members__.items():
478	            if k == value:
479	                return v
480	        raise AssertionError(f"unknown ufunc key {value}")
481	
482	
483	class DeviceCheckType(Enum):
484	    NoCheck = 0
485	    ExactSame = 1
486	
487	
488	class ViewSchemaKind(Enum):
489	    aliasing = auto()
490	    aliasing_inplace = auto()
491	    non_aliasing = auto()
492	
493	
494	# The basic input to the code generation is native_functions.yaml.
495	# The name "native", BTW, comes from the distinction between native
496	# functions and legacy TH functions.  The legacy TH functions are gone,
497	# but the "native" descriptor has stuck.
498	#
499	# NativeFunction models a single entry in native_functions.yaml.  Its
500	# fields roughly correspond to what you would see in the YAML itself,
501	# but after canonicalization and parsing has occurred.
502	#
503	# You can see some of the overall design patterns for how we setup
504	# dataclasses in this class, but we will defer a complete discussion
505	# of this at FunctionSchema.
506	@dataclass(frozen=True)
507	class NativeFunction:
508	    # The namespace for this operator. For example, if we have "at::add"
509	    # then the namespace would be "at". This enables ops to be registered
510	    # through the same DSL with a custom namespace. If not specified, the
511	    # default namespace would be "at".
512	    namespace: str
513	
514	    # The function schema of the operator in question.  This schema
515	    # has been parsed; see FunctionSchema for more about its structure.
516	    # (This type is quoted as we are forward referencing a type
517	    # defined later in the file.  I opted for this ordering of the
518	    # classes for expository clarity.)
519	    func: FunctionSchema
520	
521	    # Whether or not to generate mutable tensor arguments like regular
522	    # ones
523	    use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors: bool
524	
525	    # Whether or not to omit automatic generation of a DeviceGuard
526	    device_guard: bool
527	
528	    # How to emit automatic generation of device check
529	    device_check: DeviceCheckType
530	
531	    # What python module to put the function in
532	    python_module: str | None
533	
534	    # TODO: figure out what this does
535	    category_override: str | None
536	
537	    # If no variants are specified in native_functions.yaml, this is
538	    # assumed to be {'function'}.
539	    variants: set[Variant]
540	
541	    # Whether or not we should skip generating registrations for
542	    # this kernel.  This is a bit of a double-edged sword, as manual
543	    # registrations don't participate in codegen-based selective build!
544	    manual_kernel_registration: bool
545	
546	    # Whether or not to skip generating TensorMethod/Functions bindings
547	    # for this kernel.  Technically, this doesn't actually skip generating
548	    # the binding; instead, the binding gets generated to __dispatch_{funcname}
549	    # so you can make use of the normal binding if you need it.
550	    manual_cpp_binding: bool
551	
552	    # The location in the YAML file were this native function entry was
553	    # defined.  This is for conveniently reporting error messages!
554	    loc: Location
555	
556	    # A list of operators that are expected to be auto-generated for this NativeFunction.
557	    # Note: This list isn't actually directly used by the codegen to generate anything.
558	    # Instead, the codegen figures out what operators to generate purely based off of
559	    # function schema, and uses the autogen declarations to error check.
560	    # We expect every NativeFunction that gets auto-generated be explicitly called out
561	    # in native_functions.yaml
562	    autogen: list[OperatorName]
563	
564	    # If non-empty, this kernel is subject to ufunc codegen.
565	    # Sorted by ufunc_key
566	    ufunc_inner_loop: dict[UfuncKey, UfuncInnerLoop]
567	
568	    # Whether or not this out functions is a "structured kernel".  Structured
569	    # kernels are defined a little differently from normal kernels; in
570	    # particular, their shape checking logic is defined separately from
571	    # the kernel.  Only out functions can be structured; other functions
572	    # delegate to the out function using the structured_delegate keyword.
573	    # Every structured kernel must have at least an out and a functional
574	    # variant.
575	    structured: bool
576	
577	    # Whether or not this non-out function is a structured kernel, defined
578	    # in terms of the out kernel referenced by the string here.
579	    structured_delegate: OperatorName | None
580	
581	    # Only valid for structured kernels.  Specifies alternative of what
582	    # to inherit from when defining the meta class for the structured
583	    # operator.  This will usually be TensorIteratorBase.  This also
584	    # changes the semantics of set_output to call the parent class.
585	    structured_inherits: str | None
586	
587	    # Structured kernels can declare elements as "precomputed". These elements
588	    # are returned by the meta function in one struct and passed to the impl
589	    # function in lieu of certain kernel arguments that these precomputed
590	    # elements supersede. Information about the names and types of these
591	    # precomputed elements and how they correspond to kernel arguments is stored
592	    # in this member, if applicable.
593	    precomputed: Precompute | None
594	
595	    # Argument names whose default  should be excluded from the C++ interface.
596	    # Intended for resolving overload ambiguities between signatures.
597	    cpp_no_default_args: set[str]
598	
599	    # Note [Abstract ATen methods]
600	    # ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
601	    # An abstract ATen method is one whose dispatch differs between
602	    # types.  These are implemented in derived types (with a
603	    # standard (throwing) definition in Type).  A concrete ATen
604	    # method is one which has the same dispatch for all types;
605	    # we just implement it in the base Type.  This is exposed
606	    # in Declarations.yaml via a field named 'abstract'.
607	    is_abstract: bool
608	
609	    # Whether or not the NativeFunction contains a backend-agnostic kernel
610	    has_composite_implicit_autograd_kernel: bool
611	    has_composite_implicit_autograd_nested_tensor_kernel: bool
612	    has_composite_explicit_autograd_kernel: bool
613	    has_composite_explicit_autograd_non_functional_kernel: bool
614	
615	    # Tags are used to describe semantic information about (groups of) operators,
616	    # That aren't easily inferable directly from the operator's schema.
617	    tags: set[str]
618	
619	    # NB: The benefit of defining a dataclass is that we automatically get
620	    # a constructor defined for all the fields we specify.  No need
621	    # to explicitly write it out.
622	
623	    # We parse both the NativeFunction + backend-specific information about it, which it stored in a corresponding BackendIndex.
624	    @staticmethod
625	    def from_yaml(
626	        ei: dict[str, object],
627	        loc: Location,
628	        valid_tags: set[str],
629	        ignore_keys: set[DispatchKey] | None = None,
630	    ) -> tuple[NativeFunction, dict[DispatchKey, dict[OperatorName, BackendMetadata]]]:
631	        """
632	        Parse a NativeFunction from a dictionary as directly parsed
633	        from native_functions.yaml
634	        """
635	        e = ei.copy()
636	
637	        funcs = e.pop("func")
638	        if not isinstance(funcs, str):
639	            raise AssertionError(f"not a str: {funcs}")
640	        # only support one level of namespace. E.g., aten::add
641	        namespace_helper = NamespaceHelper.from_namespaced_entity(
642	            namespaced_entity=funcs, max_level=1
643	        )
644	        namespace = namespace_helper.get_cpp_namespace(default="aten")
645	        func = FunctionSchema.parse(namespace_helper.entity_name)
646	
647	        cpp_no_default_args_list = e.pop("cpp_no_default_args", [])
648	        if not isinstance(cpp_no_default_args_list, list):
649	            raise AssertionError(
650	                f"cpp_no_default_args is not a list: {cpp_no_default_args_list}"
651	            )
652	        cpp_no_default_args = set(cpp_no_default_args_list)
653	
654	        use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors = e.pop(
655	            "use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors", False
656	        )
657	        if not isinstance(use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors, bool):
658	            raise AssertionError(
659	                f"use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors is not a bool: {use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors}"
660	            )
661	
662	        if use_const_ref_for_mutable_tensors:
663	            if func.arguments.out:
664	                raise AssertionError(
665	                    "see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/145522"
666	                )
667	
668	        variants_s = e.pop("variants", "function")
669	        if not isinstance(variants_s, str):
670	            raise AssertionError(f"variants is not a str: {variants_s}")
671	        variants: set[Variant] = set()
672	        for v in variants_s.split(", "):
673	            if v == "function":
674	                variants.add(Variant.function)
675	            elif v == "method":
676	                variants.add(Variant.method)
677	            else:
678	                raise AssertionError(f"illegal variant {v}")
679	
680	        manual_kernel_registration = e.pop("manual_kernel_registration", False)
681	        if not isinstance(manual_kernel_registration, bool):
682	            raise AssertionError(f"not a bool: {manual_kernel_registration}")
683	
684	        manual_cpp_binding = e.pop("manual_cpp_binding", False)
685	        if not isinstance(manual_cpp_binding, bool):
686	            raise AssertionError(f"not a bool: {manual_cpp_binding}")
687	
688	        device_guard = e.pop("device_guard", True)
689	        if not isinstance(device_guard, bool):
690	            raise AssertionError(f"not a bool: {device_guard}")
691	
692	        device_check_s = e.pop("device_check", None)
693	        if not (device_check_s is None or isinstance(device_check_s, str)):
694	            raise AssertionError(f"not a str: {device_check_s}")
695	        if not (
696	            device_check_s is None or device_check_s in DeviceCheckType.__members__
697	        ):
698	            raise AssertionError(f"illegal device_check: {device_check_s}")
699	        device_check: DeviceCheckType
700	        if device_check_s is None:
701	            device_check = DeviceCheckType.ExactSame
702	        else:
703	            device_check = DeviceCheckType[device_check_s]
704	
705	        structured = e.pop("structured", False)
706	        if not isinstance(structured, bool):
707	            raise AssertionError(f"not a bool: {structured}")
708	
709	        structured_delegate_s = e.pop("structured_delegate", None)
710	        if not (
711	            structured_delegate_s is None or isinstance(structured_delegate_s, str)
712	        ):
713	            raise AssertionError(f"not a str: {structured_delegate_s}")
714	        if structured_delegate_s is not None and "::" in structured_delegate_s:
715	            raise AssertionError(
716	                "namespace is not supported in structured delegate,"
717	                " using the same namespace as the native function"
718	            )
719	        structured_delegate: OperatorName | None = None
720	        if structured_delegate_s is not None:
721	            structured_delegate = OperatorName.parse(structured_delegate_s)
722	
723	        structured_inherits = e.pop("structured_inherits", None)
724	        if not (structured_inherits is None or isinstance(structured_inherits, str)):
725	            raise AssertionError(f"not a str: {structured_inherits}")
726	        if structured_inherits is not None and "::" in structured_inherits:
727	            raise AssertionError(
728	                "namespace is not supported in structured inherits,"
729	                " using the same namespace as the native function"
730	            )
731	
732	        python_module = e.pop("python_module", None)
733	        if not (python_module is None or isinstance(python_module, str)):
734	            raise AssertionError(f"not a str: {python_module}")
735	        if python_module is not None and Variant.method in variants:
736	            raise AssertionError("functions in modules cannot be methods")
737	
738	        category_override = e.pop("category_override", None)
739	        if not (category_override is None or isinstance(category_override, str)):
740	            raise AssertionError(f"not a str: {category_override}")
741	
742	        precomputed_dict = e.pop("precomputed", None)
743	        if precomputed_dict is not None and structured is not True:
744	            raise AssertionError(
745	                f"precomputed requires structured=True, got structured={structured}"
746	            )
747	        precomputed = Precompute.parse(precomputed_dict) if precomputed_dict else None
748	
749	        tags_inp = e.pop("tags", [])
750	        if isinstance(tags_inp, str):
751	            tags_inp = [tags_inp]
752	        if not isinstance(tags_inp, list):
753	            raise AssertionError(f"tags is not a list: {tags_inp}")
754	
755	        # All aten ops generated by torchgen receive the pt2_compliant tag.
756	        if namespace == "aten" and "pt2_compliant_tag" in valid_tags:
757	            tags_inp.append("pt2_compliant_tag")
758	
759	        # All out= ops receive the "out" tag.
760	        if func.is_out_fn() and "out" in valid_tags:
761	            tags_inp.append("out")
762	
763	        # All inplace ops receive the "inplace" tag.
764	        if func.name.name.inplace and "inplace" in valid_tags:
765	            tags_inp.append("inplace")
766	
767	        tags: set[str] = set()
768	        for t in tags_inp:
769	            if len(valid_tags) == 0:
770	                raise AssertionError("valid_tags is empty")
771	            # TODO: verify that the tag is valid and has an entry in tags.yaml
772	            if t in valid_tags:
773	                tags.add(t)
774	            else:
775	                raise AssertionError(f"illegal tag {t}")
776	
777	        from torchgen.api import cpp
778	
779	        raw_dispatch = e.pop("dispatch", None)
780	        if not (raw_dispatch is None or isinstance(raw_dispatch, dict)):
781	            raise AssertionError(f"dispatch is not a dict: {e}")
782	        dispatch: dict[DispatchKey, BackendMetadata] = {}
783	        num_dispatch_keys: int = 0
784	        if raw_dispatch is not None:
785	            if manual_kernel_registration:
786	                raise AssertionError(
787	                    "cannot specify both manual_kernel_registration and dispatch; with "
788	                    "manual registration, dispatch has no effect!"
789	                )
790	            redundant_composite_implicit_autograd = False
791	            for ks, v in raw_dispatch.items():
792	                if ks == "__line__":
793	                    continue  # not worth tracking line numbers for dispatch entries
794	                if not isinstance(ks, str):
795	                    raise AssertionError(
796	                        f"illegal dispatch key '{ks}' in {raw_dispatch}"
797	                    )
798	                if not isinstance(v, str):
799	                    raise AssertionError(
800	                        f"illegal dispatch value '{v}' in {raw_dispatch}"
801	                    )
802	                for k in ks.split(","):
803	                    dispatch_key = DispatchKey.parse(k.strip())
804	                    num_dispatch_keys += 1
805	
806	                    if ignore_keys and dispatch_key in ignore_keys:
807	                        continue
808	                    if dispatch_key not in dispatch_keys:
809	                        raise AssertionError(
810	                            f"Dispatch key {dispatch_key} of kernel {v} "
811	                            "is not a supported dispatch key."
812	                        )
813	                    # We only allow at most 3 levels of namespace for kernels.
814	                    # We will append "native" to a custom kernel namespace.
815	                    namespace_helper = NamespaceHelper.from_namespaced_entity(
816	                        v, max_level=3
817	                    )
818	                    kernel_namespace = namespace_helper.get_cpp_namespace(default="at")
819	                    # Why is 'structured' included? External backends (e.g.
820	                    # XLA) opt into which ops are structured independently
821	                    # of which in-tree ops are structured
822	                    dispatch[dispatch_key] = BackendMetadata(
823	                        kernel=namespace_helper.entity_name,
824	                        structured=structured
825	                        and is_structured_dispatch_key(dispatch_key),
826	                        cpp_namespace=(kernel_namespace + "::native"),
827	                    )
828	                    if (
829	                        dispatch_key is DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd
830	                        and v == cpp.name(func)
831	                    ):
832	                        redundant_composite_implicit_autograd = True
833	
834	            # We count the number of dispatch keys which have not been ignored to prevent a dispatch table
835	            # in which all backend keys are ignored but necessarily kept, remaining compositeimplicit,
836	            # from being treated as redundant.
837	            if num_dispatch_keys == 1 and redundant_composite_implicit_autograd:
838	                raise AssertionError(
839	                    "unnecessary dispatch table for this function; just delete the dispatch "
840	                    "key entirely"
841	                )
842	            # if a function is a structured delegate, deleting the dispatch
843	            # table is NOT semantics preserving
844	            if not (
845	                structured_delegate
846	                or dispatch.keys() != {DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd}
847	                or dispatch[DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd].supports_symint()
848	                or num_dispatch_keys != 1
849	            ):
850	                raise AssertionError(
851	                    f"unexpected name for singleton CompositeImplicitAutograd dispatch entry: expected {cpp.name(func)} "
852	                    f"but got {dispatch[DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd]}.  Rename your implementation to the expected "
853	                    "name, then delete the dispatch table"
854	                )
855	        elif not structured and structured_delegate is None:
856	            name = str(func.name.name)
857	            if (
858	                name.startswith("new_")
859	                or name.endswith("_like")
860	                # TODO: maybe it's better to test the return
861	                or (
862	                    func.arguments.tensor_options
863	                    and not func.arguments.has_tensor_arg()
864	                )
865	            ):
866	                raise AssertionError(
867	                    f"expected {name} to have a CompositeExplicitAutograd "
868	                    "dispatch entry, but there was no dispatch table.  Factory functions "
869	                    "should not have implicit dispatch as they should not be decomposed "
870	                    "for __torch_dispatch__"
871	                )
872	            dispatch[DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd] = BackendMetadata(
873	                cpp.name(func), structured=False, cpp_namespace=DEFAULT_KERNEL_NAMESPACE
874	            )
875	
876	        composites_in_dispatch = [
877	            d
878	            for d in dispatch
879	            if d == DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutograd
880	            or d == DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutogradNonFunctional
881	            or d == DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd
882	            or d == DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutogradNestedTensor
883	        ]
884	
885	        if not (
886	            len(composites_in_dispatch) <= 1
887	            or (
888	                len(composites_in_dispatch) == 2
889	                and (
890	                    DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutogradNonFunctional
891	                    not in composites_in_dispatch
892	                )
893	                and (
894	                    DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutogradNestedTensor
895	                    in composites_in_dispatch
896	                )
897	            )
898	        ):
899	            raise AssertionError(
900	                "cannot specify more than one of CompositeExplicitAutograd, CompositeExplicitAutogradNonFunctional, "
901	                "or CompositeImplicitAutograd on a single kernel; each "
902	                "strictly subsumes the other.  If you wanted to provide an explicit autograd "
903	                "implementation, specify CompositeExplicitAutograd; otherwise specify CompositeImplicitAutograd only"
904	            )
905	
906	        autogen_str = e.pop("autogen", "")
907	        if not isinstance(autogen_str, str):
908	            raise AssertionError(f"autogen is not a str: {autogen_str}")
909	        autogen = (
910	            []
911	            if autogen_str == ""
912	            else [OperatorName.parse(x) for x in autogen_str.split(", ")]
913	        )
914	
915	        raw_ufunc_inner_loop = e.pop("ufunc_inner_loop", {})
916	        ufunc_inner_loop = {}
917	        if isinstance(raw_ufunc_inner_loop, str):
918	            ufunc_inner_loop[UfuncKey.Generic] = UfuncInnerLoop.parse(
919	                raw_ufunc_inner_loop, UfuncKey.Generic
920	            )
921	        elif isinstance(raw_ufunc_inner_loop, dict):
922	            for k, vo in raw_ufunc_inner_loop.items():
923	                if k == "__line__":
924	                    continue
925	                if not isinstance(k, str):
926	                    raise AssertionError(f"ufunc_inner_loop key is not a str: {k}")
927	                if not isinstance(vo, str):
928	                    raise AssertionError(f"ufunc_inner_loop value is not a str: {vo}")
929	                ufunc_key = UfuncKey.parse(k)
930	                ufunc_inner_loop[ufunc_key] = UfuncInnerLoop.parse(vo, ufunc_key)
931	        else:
932	            raise AssertionError(
933	                f"ufunc_inner_loop not str or dict: {raw_ufunc_inner_loop}"
934	            )
935	        # Program the BackendIndex for the implicit dispatch entry from ufunc
936	        if ufunc_inner_loop:
937	            if not structured:
938	                raise AssertionError("ufunc must be structured")
939	
940	            # Delay import ufunc here to avoid circular import issue
941	            # See: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/81294
942	            import torchgen.api.ufunc as ufunc
943	
944	            for dispatch_key in UFUNC_DISPATCH_KEYS:
945	                if dispatch_key in dispatch:
946	                    raise AssertionError(
947	                        f"ufunc should not have explicit dispatch entry for {dispatch_key}"
948	                    )
949	                dispatch[dispatch_key] = BackendMetadata(
950	                    kernel=ufunc.schema_kernel_name(func, dispatch_key),
951	                    structured=True,
952	                    cpp_namespace=DEFAULT_KERNEL_NAMESPACE,
953	                )
954	
955	        if structured_delegate:
956	            # Structured functions MUST have a dispatch table
957	            is_abstract = True
958	        else:
959	            is_abstract = (
960	                dispatch.keys() != {DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd}
961	                and dispatch.keys()
962	                != {DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutogradNestedTensor}
963	                and dispatch.keys()
964	                != {
965	                    DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd,
966	                    DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutogradNestedTensor,
967	                }
968	            )
969	
970	        has_composite_implicit_autograd_kernel = (
971	            DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutograd in dispatch
972	        )
973	        has_composite_implicit_autograd_nested_tensor_kernel = (
974	            DispatchKey.CompositeImplicitAutogradNestedTensor in dispatch
975	        )
976	        has_composite_explicit_autograd_kernel = (
977	            DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutograd in dispatch
978	        )

1021	                loc=loc,

1024	                has_composite_implicit_autograd_kernel=has_composite_implicit_autograd_kernel,

1034	    def validate_unstructured(self) -> None:

1036	        # at once

1038	            raise AssertionError(
1039	                "This function is structured, but there was "
1040	                "no valid functional variant of it."
1041	            )
1042	        if not self.structured_delegate:
1043	            raise AssertionError(
1044	                "This function delegates to another structured out function, "
1045	                "but no valid function was found (the delegate may not exist, or it has the wrong type)"
1046	            )

1048	    # __post_init__ functions in dataclasses can be used to do extra
1049	    # validation after construction.

1051	    # Notice that we don't do any type validation here.  In fact, we
1052	    # rely exclusively on mypy to check if you've done types correctly!
1053	    # Validation is for nontrivial invariants that cannot be (conveniently)
1054	    # encoded in the type system.
1055	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
1056	        if self.func.arguments.out:
1057	            if self.variants != {Variant.function}:
1058	                raise AssertionError(
1059	                    "Native functions with out arguments MUST "
1060	                    "be declared with only function variant; e.g., variants: function; "
1061	                    "otherwise you will tickle a Python argument binding bug "
1062	                    "(which usually manifests itself as the result variable being undefined.)"
1063	                )
1064	        if self.structured:
1065	            if self.func.kind() != SchemaKind.out:
1066	                raise AssertionError(
1067	                    "Put structured field on the out= "
1068	                    "variant of a function; did you mean structured_delegate?"
1069	                )
1070	            if not self.device_guard:
1071	                raise AssertionError(
1072	                    "device_guard: False is not respected by structured kernels"
1073	                )
1074	        if self.structured_delegate:
1075	            if self.func.kind() == SchemaKind.out:
1076	                raise AssertionError(
1077	                    "structured_delegate field not allowed "
1078	                    "on out= functions; did you mean structured?"
1079	                )
1080	            if not self.device_guard:
1081	                raise AssertionError(
1082	                    "device_guard: False is not respected by structured kernels"
1083	                )
1084	        # Technically, with the asserts above, this assert is impossible to
1085	        # happen
1086	        if self.structured and self.structured_delegate:
1087	            raise AssertionError(
1088	                "Cannot have both structured and structured_delegate on function"
1089	            )
1090	        defaulted_arguments = {
1091	            a.name for a in self.func.schema_order_arguments() if a.default is not None
1092	        }
1093	        invalid_args = set.difference(self.cpp_no_default_args, defaulted_arguments)
1094	        if len(invalid_args) != 0:
1095	            raise AssertionError(f"Invalid cpp_no_default_args: {invalid_args}")
1096	        if self.structured_inherits is not None:
1097	            if not self.structured:
1098	                raise AssertionError(
1099	                    "structured_inherits must also imply structured: True"
1100	                )
1101	        if str(self.func.name).startswith("_foreach"):
1102	            if self.device_check != DeviceCheckType.NoCheck:
1103	                raise AssertionError(
1104	                    "foreach kernels fall back to slow path when tensor are on different devices, "
1105	                    "device_check not allowed to be enabled"
1106	                )
1107	
1108	        # NB: if your function accidentally has rand/dropout/... in its name
1109	        # but is not actually random, feel free to amend this to special case
1110	        if (
1111	            "rand" in str(self.func.name)
1112	            or (
1113	                (
1114	                    "dropout" in str(self.func.name)
1115	                    or any(
1116	                        "dropout" in arg.name for arg in self.func.arguments.flat_all
1117	                    )
1118	                )
1119	                # Backwards of dropout is typically deterministic
1120	                and "backward" not in str(self.func.name)
1121	                and str(self.func.name.name) != "_cudnn_init_dropout_state"
1122	            )
1123	            or self.func.arguments.has_generator_arg()
1124	        ):
1125	            if "nondeterministic_seeded" not in self.tags:

1187	# A structured kernel is guaranteed to have a functional and out variant, and
1188	# optionally an inplace variant.
1189	#
1190	# NB: we create NativeFunctionsGroup *even if* the function is not
1191	# actually annotated structured.  Test the structured boolean to see if it
1192	# actually is structured or not.

1194	class NativeFunctionsGroup:
1195	    functional: NativeFunction
1196	    inplace: NativeFunction | None

1198	    out: NativeFunction
1199	
1200	    @property
1201	    def structured(self) -> bool:
1202	        # Whether or not the operator has a meta() function. This information is backend-agnostic.
1203	        return self.out.structured
1204	
1205	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
1206	        test_sig: FunctionSchema = self.functional.func.signature()
1207	        for f in self.functions():
1208	            if test_sig != f.func.signature():
1209	                raise AssertionError(
1210	                    "NativeFunctionsGroup constructed from two NativeFunctions "
1211	                    f"that don't have matching signatures: {test_sig} != {f.func.signature()}"
1212	                )
1213	
1214	            if self.structured != f.part_of_structured_group:
1215	                raise AssertionError(
1216	                    "NativeFunctionsGroup constructed from structured and unstructured "
1217	                    f"functions: {self.out.func.name} and {f.func.name}"
1218	                )
1219	        if self.functional.func.kind() != SchemaKind.functional:
1220	            raise AssertionError(
1221	                f"functional.func.kind() is {self.functional.func.kind()}, expected SchemaKind.functional"
1222	            )
1223	        if self.out.func.kind() != SchemaKind.out:
1224	            raise AssertionError(
1225	                f"out.func.kind() is {self.out.func.kind()}, expected SchemaKind.out"
1226	            )
1227	        if self.functional.namespace != self.out.namespace:
1228	            raise AssertionError(
1229	                f"functional.namespace ({self.functional.namespace}) != out.namespace ({self.out.namespace})"
1230	            )
1231	        if self.inplace is not None:
1232	            if self.inplace.func.kind() != SchemaKind.inplace:
1233	                raise AssertionError(
1234	                    f"inplace.func.kind() is {self.inplace.func.kind()}, expected SchemaKind.inplace"
1235	                )
1236	            if self.inplace.namespace != self.functional.namespace:
1237	                raise AssertionError(
1238	                    f"inplace.namespace ({self.inplace.namespace}) != functional.namespace ({self.functional.namespace})"
1239	                )
1240	
1241	        if self.mutable is not None:
1242	            if self.mutable.func.kind() != SchemaKind.mutable:
1243	                raise AssertionError(
1244	                    f"mutable.func.kind() is {self.mutable.func.kind()}, expected SchemaKind.mutable"
1245	                )
1246	            if self.mutable.namespace != self.functional.namespace:
1247	                raise AssertionError(
1248	                    f"mutable.namespace ({self.mutable.namespace}) != functional.namespace ({self.functional.namespace})"
1249	                )
1250	            # See Note [Overload Ambiguity With Functional Variants]
1251	            if not self.functional.func.name.name.functional_overload:
1252	                raise AssertionError(
1253	                    "functional.func.name.name.functional_overload must be True when mutable is not None"
1254	                )
1255	
1256	        if self.structured:
1257	            # For now, structured composite kernels are not supported (need some
1258	            # design work to figure out how to make the composite case work)
1259	            if (

1261	                or self.out.has_composite_implicit_autograd_nested_tensor_kernel
1262	            ):

1264	
1265	            if self.functional.structured_delegate != self.out.func.name:
1266	                raise AssertionError(
1267	                    f"{self.functional.func.name} delegates to {self.functional.structured_delegate} "
1268	                    f"but its actual delegate is {self.out.func.name}"
1269	                )
1270	            if self.inplace is not None:
1271	                if self.inplace.structured_delegate != self.out.func.name:
1272	                    raise AssertionError(
1273	                        f"{self.inplace.func.name} delegates to {self.inplace.structured_delegate} "
1274	                        f"but its actual delegate is {self.out.func.name}"
1275	                    )
1276	
1277	        generated_fns = sorted(
1278	            [str(f.func.name) for f in self.functions() if "generated" in f.tags]
1279	        )
1280	        generated_fns_str = ", ".join(str(x) for x in generated_fns)
1281	        expected_generated_fns: set[str] = set()
1282	        for f in self.functions():
1283	            expected_generated_fns.update(str(op) for op in f.autogen)
1284	        expected_generated_fns_str = ", ".join(
1285	            str(x) for x in sorted(expected_generated_fns)
1286	        )
1287	        if len(expected_generated_fns) == 0 and len(generated_fns) > 0:
1288	            raise RuntimeError(
1289	                f"The codegen expects to be able to generate '{generated_fns_str}'."
1290	                " In order to generate them however, we expect them to be called out explicitly in the yaml."

1292	            )
1293	        if expected_generated_fns_str != generated_fns_str:
1294	            raise RuntimeError(
1295	                f"The codegen expects to be able to generate '{generated_fns_str}'."
1296	                f" To do so, it expects a line: 'autogen: {generated_fns_str}'."

1298	            )

1303	    def functions(self) -> Iterator[NativeFunction]:
1304	        yield self.functional
1305	        yield self.out
1306	        if self.inplace is not None:
1307	            yield self.inplace
1308	        if self.mutable is not None:

1315	    @staticmethod
1316	    def from_dict(d: dict[SchemaKind, NativeFunction]) -> NativeFunctionsGroup | None:
1317	        if not d:
1318	            raise AssertionError("from_dict called with empty dict")
1319	        if len(d) == 1:
1320	            return None
1321	        d = dict(d)  # non-destructive updates please
1322	        functional = d.pop(SchemaKind.functional, None)
1323	        inplace = d.pop(SchemaKind.inplace, None)
1324	        mutable = d.pop(SchemaKind.mutable, None)
1325	        out = d.pop(SchemaKind.out, None)
1326	        if d:
1327	            raise AssertionError(f"unexpected keys in dict: {d}")
1328	        if functional is None:
1329	            raise AssertionError("functional variant is required")

1331	        # these don't count as structured for our purposes here
1332	        if out is None:
1333	            return None
1334	        # assuming all variants have the same namespace
1335	        return NativeFunctionsGroup(
1336	            functional=functional,
1337	            inplace=inplace,
1338	            mutable=mutable,
1339	            out=out,
1340	        )

1366	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1367	class UfuncInnerLoop:
1368	    name: str
1369	    supported_dtypes: OrderedSet[ScalarType]
1370	    # key is stored here because it affects the semantics of name,
1371	    # so its helpful to have them together for further processing
1372	    ufunc_key: UfuncKey
1373	
1374	    @staticmethod
1375	    def parse(value: str, ufunc_key: UfuncKey) -> UfuncInnerLoop:
1376	        name, supported_dtypes_str = value.split(" ", 1)
1377	        if supported_dtypes_str[0] != "(":
1378	            raise AssertionError(
1379	                f"expected '(' at start of supported_dtypes, got: {supported_dtypes_str}"
1380	            )
1381	        if supported_dtypes_str[-1] != ")":
1382	            raise AssertionError(
1383	                f"expected ')' at end of supported_dtypes, got: {supported_dtypes_str}"
1384	            )
1385	        supported_dtypes: OrderedSet[ScalarType] = OrderedSet()
1386	        for k in supported_dtypes_str[1:-1].split(", "):
1387	            supported_dtypes |= ScalarType.parse_set(k)
1388	        return UfuncInnerLoop(
1389	            name=name, supported_dtypes=supported_dtypes, ufunc_key=ufunc_key
1390	        )

1393	# BackendIndex represents a backend.
1394	# The BackendIndex encodes per-operator information that is potentially different
1395	# for each backend. The most obvious example is the name of the kernel
1396	# (the 'dispatch' entry in native_functions.yaml).
1397	# However, there can be other examples of different backends having different information.
1398	# External backends can choose to opt their kernels to be structured independently from in-tree backends,
1399	# which means that this information isn't inherently tied to a NativeFunction- it's different per backend.
1400	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1401	class BackendIndex:
1402	    dispatch_key: DispatchKey
1403	    # Mainly important for structured kernels, this determines which variant in the operator group is used to implement the others.
1404	    # All in-tree ops use out kernels, while XLA uses functional kernels.
1405	    use_out_as_primary: bool
1406	    # Whether the backend requires a device guard, and device checks.
1407	    # For in-tree backends, this is currently just CUDA/HIP
1408	    # For out-of-tree backends, this is currently just Intel XPU
1409	    device_guard: bool
1410	    # Whether the backend is in-tree (CPU/CUDA) or out-of-tree (XLA)
1411	    external: bool
1412	    # Other backend-specific information that is on a per-operator basis
1413	    index: dict[OperatorName, BackendMetadata]
1414	
1415	    @staticmethod
1416	    def grow_index(
1417	        parent_index: dict[DispatchKey, dict[OperatorName, BackendMetadata]],
1418	        child_index: dict[DispatchKey, dict[OperatorName, BackendMetadata]],
1419	    ) -> None:
1420	        for k, v in child_index.items():
1421	            for op_name, metadata in v.items():
1422	                if op_name in parent_index[k]:
1423	                    raise AssertionError(
1424	                        f"duplicate operator {op_name} for dispatch key {k}"
1425	                    )
1426	                parent_index[k][op_name] = metadata
1427	
1428	    def primary(self, g: NativeFunctionsGroup) -> NativeFunction:
1429	        if self.use_out_as_primary:
1430	            return g.out
1431	        else:
1432	            return g.functional
1433	
1434	    def has_kernel(self, g: NativeFunction | NativeFunctionsGroup) -> bool:
1435	        m = self.get_kernel(g)
1436	        return m is not None
1437	
1438	    def get_kernel(
1439	        self, g: NativeFunction | NativeFunctionsGroup
1440	    ) -> BackendMetadata | None:
1441	        if isinstance(g, NativeFunction):
1442	            f = g
1443	        elif isinstance(g, NativeFunctionsGroup):
1444	            f = self.primary(g)
1445	        else:
1446	            assert_never(g)
1447	        if f.func.name not in self.index:
1448	            return None
1449	        return self.index[f.func.name]
1450	
1451	    def native_function_class_name(self) -> str | None:
1452	        if self.external:
1453	            return f"{str(self.dispatch_key)}NativeFunctions"
1454	        else:
1455	            # TODO: This discrepancy isn't required; we could also generated
1456	            # a class for in-tree kernels. It'll just require carefully
1457	            # updating every kernel definition + callsite of every in-tree aten kernel.
1458	            return None

1461	# The function schema is undoubtedly the most important data structure
1462	# in all of the codegen, as it defines the type signature for operators,
1463	# and most of the code generation we do is type directed (e.g., look at
1464	# the types, decide what to do.  Think about how we code generate
1465	# C++ function stubs!)
1466	#
1467	# We will also see in this class the general structure for how we model
1468	# data in this code generation.  A few notable properties to point out
1469	# ahead of time:
1470	#
1471	#   - These dataclasses are a *lossless* representation of the strings
1472	#     they are parsed from.  In fact, we assert that given the
1473	#     information stored in the dataclass, we can exactly reconstruct
1474	#     the string we parsed from (and assert this inside the parse
1475	#     definition).  There are a few reasons for this:
1476	#
1477	#       - If you find that it is difficult to reconstruct the string
1478	#         given a dataclass, that is a clue that you are data
1479	#         representation is wrong.
1480	#
1481	#       - It helps ensure that all relevant information is present
1482	#         in the dataclass, so that downstream users aren't tempted
1483	#         to reparse the original string to get some information
1484	#         that was omitted.
1485	#
1486	#       - It forces you to represent the data in-memory in the same way
1487	#         it is recorded textually, which makes the dataclasses easier
1488	#         to understand for someone who is familiar with the
1489	#         textual format.  (As a tradeoff, it means you have to model
1490	#         the syntax, even when it is inconvenient.  But maybe that means
1491	#         the syntax is bad!)  If you don't understand the internal
1492	#         representation, go look at the printing code to see how
1493	#         it maps onto the surface syntax!
1494	#
1495	#       - It makes it easy to test the parsing code, as parsing code
1496	#         that is inconsistent with the string code will fail early
1497	#         and loudly.  (As a tradeoff, it makes the parsing code a bit
1498	#         brittle (in particular, with trivial whitespace changes you
1499	#         are likely to trigger an assert error).
1500	#
1501	#     In general, try to make the __str__ code as simple as possible
1502	#     (even at the cost of more complex parsing logic.)  Additionally,
1503	#     try to minimize redundancy in data representation.  (Precomputed
1504	#     fields are OK though: they are defined as a simple function on
1505	#     the canonical representation in question.)
1506	#
1507	#   - These dataclasses are all frozen; once constructed their
1508	#     values never change.  This makes it easy to tell where any
1509	#     given data came from: just look to the constructor.  As a
1510	#     tradeoff, you can't easily "decorate" a schema with extra
1511	#     information from a post-facto analysis.  We impose this
1512	#     restriction to make these structures more understandable.
1513	#
1514	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1515	class FunctionSchema:
1516	    # The name of the operator this function schema describes.
1517	    name: OperatorName
1518	
1519	    arguments: Arguments
1520	
1521	    # TODO: Need to handle collisions with argument names at some point
1522	    returns: tuple[Return, ...]
1523	
1524	    @property
1525	    def is_mutable(self) -> bool:
1526	        def is_write(arg: Argument) -> bool:
1527	            if arg.annotation is None:
1528	                return False
1529	            return arg.annotation.is_write
1530	
1531	        # Corresponds to torch._C._FunctionSchema.is_mutable
1532	        # See aten/src/ATen/core/function_schema.h (keep these in sync)
1533	        return any(is_write(a) for a in self.arguments.flat_all)
1534	
1535	    def schema_order_arguments(self) -> Iterator[Argument]:
1536	        return itertools.chain(
1537	            self.arguments.flat_positional,
1538	            self.arguments.flat_kwarg_only,
1539	            self.arguments.out,
1540	        )
1541	
1542	    decl_re = re.compile(r"(?P<name>[^\(]+)\((?P<args>.*)\) -> (?P<returns>.*)")
1543	
1544	    @staticmethod
1545	    def parse(func: str) -> FunctionSchema:
1546	        # We should probably get a proper parser here
1547	        decls = FunctionSchema.decl_re.findall(func)
1548	        if len(decls) != 1:
1549	            raise AssertionError(f"Invalid function schema: {func}")
1550	        ops, args, return_decl = decls[0]
1551	        name = OperatorName.parse(ops)
1552	        arguments = Arguments.parse(args)
1553	        returns = parse_returns(return_decl)
1554	        r = FunctionSchema(name=name, arguments=arguments, returns=returns)
1555	        if str(r) != func:
1556	            raise AssertionError(f"{str(r)} != {func}")
1557	        return r
1558	
1559	    def returns_are_aliased(self) -> bool:
1560	        # We assert earlier that schemas can't have a mix of aliased and non-aliased returns
1561	        return any(
1562	            r
1563	            for r in self.returns
1564	            if r.annotation is not None and r.annotation.is_write
1565	        )
1566	
1567	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
1568	        for arg, ret in zip(self.arguments.out, self.returns):
1569	            if arg.annotation != ret.annotation:
1570	                raise AssertionError(
1571	                    "Out arguments must have matching return Tensor; furthermore, "
1572	                    f"the ith-argument needs to correspond to the ith return. "
1573	                    f"arg.annotation={arg.annotation}, ret.annotation={ret.annotation}"
1574	                )
1575	        # We also enforce that if you have any mutable, positional args, then they are not returned.
1576	        # This makes it easier to group these functions properly with their functional/out= counterparts.
1577	        for a in self.arguments.post_self_positional_mutable:
1578	            if any(a.annotation == r.annotation for r in self.returns):
1579	                raise AssertionError(
1580	                    f"If you have a schema with mutable positional args, we expect them to not be returned. schema: {str(self)}"
1581	                )
1582	        # Invariant: we expect out arguments to appear as keyword arguments in the schema.
1583	        # This means that all mutable returns should be aliased to a keyword argument
1584	        # (except for "self", which we explicitly don't treat as an out argument because of its use in methods)
1585	        # See Note [is_out_fn]
1586	        out_and_self = list(self.arguments.out) + [
1587	            arg for arg in self.arguments.flat_positional if arg.name == "self"
1588	        ]
1589	        mutable_returns = [
1590	            ret
1591	            for ret in self.returns
1592	            if ret.annotation is not None and ret.annotation.is_write
1593	        ]
1594	        immutable_returns = [
1595	            ret
1596	            for ret in self.returns
1597	            if ret.annotation is None or not ret.annotation.is_write
1598	        ]
1599	        # Some assertions: We don't want any functions with a return type of "-> (Tensor(a!), Tensor)",
1600	        # because:
1601	        # (1) It's more annoying to handle properly
1602	        # (2) It's unnecessary - you can't method-chain on the first (mutated) output because it's part of a tuple.
1603	        # Instead, we expect the (a!) argument to not be returned.
1604	        if not (len(mutable_returns) == 0 or len(immutable_returns) == 0):
1605	            raise AssertionError(
1606	                f"NativeFunctions must have either only mutable returns, or only immutable returns. Found: {str(self)}"
1607	            )
1608	        for ret in mutable_returns:
1609	            if not any(ret.annotation == arg.annotation for arg in out_and_self):
1610	                raise AssertionError(
1611	                    'All mutable returns must be aliased either to a keyword argument, or to "self". '
1612	                    "Did you forget to mark an out argument as keyword-only?"
1613	                )
1614	        if self.arguments.out:
1615	            # out= ops that return their mutable inputs are only really useful for method chaining.
1616	            # And method chaining is only really useful if the thing you're returning is a plain Tensor.
1617	            # So ideally, we'd enforce that out= ops with a single plain mutable tensor should return the tensor,
1618	            # and all other types of out= op schemas should return void.
1619	            # There are a bunch of existing out= ops that return tuples of tensors though, so we're stuck with allowing that.
1620	            if any(a.type != BaseType(BaseTy.Tensor) for a in self.arguments.out):
1621	                if len(self.returns) != 0:
1622	                    raise AssertionError(
1623	                        "out= ops that accept tensor lists as out arguments "
1624	                        "are expected to have no return type (since you can't do method chaining on them)"
1625	                    )
1626	            else:
1627	                # mutable keyword arguments whose name has _scratch_ prefix are
1628	                # scratch tensors for memory planning and should not be returned
1629	                non_scratch_out_args = len(
1630	                    [
1631	                        arg
1632	                        for arg in self.arguments.out
1633	                        if not arg.name.startswith("_scratch_")
1634	                    ]
1635	                )
1636	                if non_scratch_out_args != len(self.returns):
1637	                    raise AssertionError(
1638	                        f"Must return as many arguments as there are out arguments, or no return at all. "
1639	                        f"Got {non_scratch_out_args} non-scratch out args and {len(self.returns)} returns"
1640	                    )
1641	
1642	        if self.name.name.inplace:
1643	            self_a = self.arguments.self_arg
1644	            if not (
1645	                self_a
1646	                and self_a.argument.annotation
1647	                and self_a.argument.annotation.is_write
1648	            ):
1649	                raise AssertionError(
1650	                    f"Inplace op {self.name} must have a self argument with a mutable annotation"
1651	                )
1652	            if self_a.argument.type == BaseType(BaseTy.Tensor):
1653	                # All inplace ops with an ordinary `Tensor self` argument should return self,
1654	                # to allow for method chaining.
1655	                if not (
1656	                    len(self.returns) == 1
1657	                    and self.returns[0].annotation == self_a.argument.annotation
1658	                ):
1659	                    raise AssertionError(
1660	                        f"Inplace op {self.name} with Tensor self must return self"
1661	                    )
1662	            else:
1663	                # You can't method chain on non-tensor self arguments though (like a list[Tensor])
1664	                # so in all other cases we expect the return type to be none.
1665	                if len(self.returns) != 0:
1666	                    raise AssertionError(
1667	                        f"Inplace op {self.name} with non-Tensor self must have no returns"
1668	                    )
1669	
1670	        if self.arguments.tensor_options is not None:
1671	            if self.kind() != SchemaKind.functional:
1672	                raise AssertionError(
1673	                    "Found an operator that is not functional or out variant, but has tensor options arguments."
1674	                    "This is not allowed- tensor options arguments are only allowed for factory functions."
1675	                    f"schema: {str(self)}"
1676	                )
1677	        if self.is_functional_fn():
1678	            if self.kind() != SchemaKind.functional:
1679	                raise AssertionError(
1680	                    "Found an operator that is not functional, but its overload contains the string 'functional'."
1681	                    "This is a special keyword in the codegen, please use a different overload name."
1682	                    f"schema: {str(self)}"
1683	                )
1684	
1685	    def is_functional_fn(self) -> bool:
1686	        return "functional" in self.name.overload_name
1687	
1688	    def is_out_fn(self) -> bool:
1689	        # Note [is_out_fn]
1690	        #
1691	        # out functions are the variants which take an explicit out= argument
1692	        # to populate into.  We need to know if a schema corresponds to an
1693	        # out function for several reasons:
1694	        #
1695	        #   - They codegen differently in C++ API
1696	        #       - codegen to at::add_out rather than at::add
1697	        #       - out argument is moved to front of C++ argument list
1698	        #
1699	        # out functions are DEFINED to be any function with a keyword-only
1700	        # argument that is mutable.  In principle, this could lead to a
1701	        # false positive if you define a function that mutates a
1702	        # kwarg only argument, but this isn't the "true" output of this
1703	        # function.  A more robust definition that would work in this
1704	        # case would also look at:
1705	        #
1706	        #   - The output types.  Out functions take in the arguments
1707	        #     they mutate and then return them again; this is sort
1708	        #     of "definitionally" what makes something an out function.
1709	        #     Historically, we DO check this for consistency.
1710	        #   - Correspondence with pure variant.  An out function
1711	        #     should have a signature equivalent to its pure variant,
1712	        #     but just with extra kwargs for the output elements.  This
1713	        #     is difficult to actually check for and historically
1714	        #     we only do this check in tools/
1715	        return bool(self.arguments.out)
1716	
1717	    def kind(self) -> SchemaKind:
1718	        """
1719	        What kind of schema is this?  A functional schema is one
1720	        that returns a newly allocated output; an inplace schema
1721	        modifies the self argument inplace; an out schema writes
1722	        the result into an explicitly provided out argument.
1723	        """
1724	        is_out = bool(self.arguments.out)
1725	        is_scratch = bool(
1726	            [arg for arg in self.arguments.out if arg.name.startswith("_scratch_")]
1727	        )
1728	        is_inplace = self.name.name.inplace
1729	        is_mutable = any(
1730	            a.annotation is not None and a.annotation.is_write
1731	            for a in self.arguments.post_self_positional
1732	        )
1733	        if is_out and is_inplace:
1734	            raise AssertionError("A schema cannot be both out= and inplace")
1735	        # out= and inplace schemas can also have post_self_positional mutable args,
1736	        # but we give precedence to out= and inplace when deciding the schema kind.
1737	        # Tradeoff: we probably don't want to have to teach codegen that looks at inplace ops
1738	        # to also worry about mutable post_self_positional arguments,
1739	        # but it seems like a much bigger lift to classify them has having a new schema kind.
1740	        # The number of ops that fit in this strange category is small enough that
1741	        # we can probably manually write code for them instead of forcing the codegen to handle them.
1742	        if is_inplace:
1743	            return SchemaKind.inplace
1744	        elif is_scratch:
1745	            if not is_out:
1746	                raise AssertionError(
1747	                    "invariant: all scratch operators are expected to be out= operators too"
1748	                )
1749	            return SchemaKind.scratch
1750	        elif is_out:
1751	            if is_scratch:
1752	                raise AssertionError(
1753	                    "We should not categorize a scratch op as an out variant. Check if the order of if statements are expected!"
1754	                )
1755	            return SchemaKind.out
1756	        elif is_mutable:
1757	            return SchemaKind.mutable
1758	        else:
1759	            return SchemaKind.functional
1760	
1761	    # For every return:
1762	    # - If the return aliases an input, we return the input name
1763	    # - Otherwise, we return None.
1764	    # If return names were enforced to be consistent with aliasing information, then we wouldn't need this.
1765	    def aliased_return_names(self) -> list[str | None]:
1766	        outs: list[str | None] = []
1767	        for r in self.returns:
1768	            aliased_args = [
1769	                a
1770	                for a in self.arguments.flat_all
1771	                if a.annotation is not None and a.annotation == r.annotation
1772	            ]
1773	            if len(aliased_args) == 0:
1774	                outs.append(None)
1775	            elif len(aliased_args) == 1:
1776	                outs.append(aliased_args[0].name)
1777	            else:
1778	                aliased_names = ", ".join(a.name for a in aliased_args)
1779	                raise AssertionError(
1780	                    f"Found a return ({r.name})that aliases multiple inputs ({aliased_names})"
1781	                )
1782	        return outs
1783	
1784	    def signature(
1785	        self,
1786	        *,
1787	        strip_default: bool = False,
1788	        strip_view_copy_name: bool = False,
1789	        keep_return_names: bool = False,
1790	    ) -> FunctionSchema:
1791	        """
1792	                Certain schemas are 'related', in that they are simply
1793	                inplace/out/functional versions of the same function.  This method
1794	                factors these schemas into the "core" functional signature which
1795	                is equal across all versions.
1796	
1797	                Here is what normalization happens to the schema to convert
1798	                it to a signature:
1799	                - The overload name is stripped (name is retained, since
1800	                  it expresses semantic content about what the function does)
1801	                - Inplace is set False
1802	                - Out arguments are stripped
1803	                - Mutable post_self_positional args are converted to returns
1804	                - Mutability annotations are stripped  (this is sound
1805	                  because you cannot overload on mutability annotation)
1806	                - Return names are stripped since they are not overloadable and
1807	                  some variants have return names but some not
1808	                - TensorOptions are dropped
1809	                  because out= variants of factory functions don't include them
1810	                  (and we want to be able to pair up factory functions with their out variants)
1811	
1812	                Finally, we want to be able to pair up related "view" and their
1813	                corresponding "view_copy" operators. We do this by optionally
1814	                stripping the trailing "_copy" from the base name.
1815	
1816	                Example of a mutable op before and after:
1817	
1818	                f.func (Mutable operator):
1819	        _fused_moving_avg_obs_fq_helper(Tensor self, Tensor observer_on, Tensor fake_quant_on, Tensor(a!) running_min, Tensor(b!) running_max, Tensor(c!) scale, Tensor(d!) zero_point, float averaging_const, int quant_min, int quant_max, int ch_axis, bool per_row_fake_quant=False, bool symmetric_quant=False) -> (Tensor output, Tensor mask)  # noqa: B950
1820	
1821	                f.func (Corresponding functional operator):
1822	        _fused_moving_avg_obs_fq_helper.functional(Tensor self, Tensor observer_on, Tensor fake_quant_on, Tensor running_min, Tensor running_max, Tensor scale, Tensor zero_point, float averaging_const, int quant_min, int quant_max, int ch_axis, bool per_row_fake_quant=False, bool symmetric_quant=False) -> (Tensor output, Tensor mask, Tensor running_min_out, Tensor running_max_out, Tensor scale_out, Tensor zero_point_out)  # noqa: B950
1823	
1824	                f.func.signature() output:
1825	        _fused_moving_avg_obs_fq_helper(Tensor self, Tensor observer_on, Tensor fake_quant_on, Tensor running_min, Tensor running_max, Tensor scale, Tensor zero_point, float averaging_const, int quant_min, int quant_max, int ch_axis, bool per_row_fake_quant=False, bool symmetric_quant=False) -> (Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor)  # noqa: B950
1826	        """
1827	
1828	        def strip_ret_annotation(r: Return) -> Return:
1829	            return Return(
1830	                name=r.name if keep_return_names else None,
1831	                type=r.type,
1832	                annotation=None,
1833	            )
1834	
1835	        base_name = self.name.name.base
1836	        if strip_view_copy_name:
1837	            if base_name.endswith("_copy"):
1838	                base_name = base_name.replace("_copy", "")
1839	            elif base_name.endswith("_scatter"):
1840	                base_name = base_name.replace("scatter", "inverse")
1841	
1842	        # find mutable inputs that are not originally returned, and convert them to returns
1843	        returns_from_mutable_inputs = tuple(
1844	            # When we're grouping functions we strip the return names,
1845	            # but when we're generating the actual functional variants then we follow
1846	            # a convention for what to name the returns
1847	            Return(
1848	                name=f"{a.name}_out" if keep_return_names else None,
1849	                type=a.type,
1850	                annotation=None,
1851	            )
1852	            for a in itertools.chain(
1853	                # Order is important here (otherwise e.g. inplace with mutable args
1854	                # and out= with mutable args won't have the same signature)
1855	                (
1856	                    [self.arguments.self_arg.argument]
1857	                    if self.arguments.self_arg is not None
1858	                    else []
1859	                ),
1860	                self.arguments.out,
1861	                self.arguments.post_self_positional,
1862	            )
1863	            if a.annotation is not None
1864	            and a.annotation.is_write
1865	            and not any(a.annotation == r.annotation for r in self.returns)
1866	        )
1867	        original_returns = tuple(map(strip_ret_annotation, self.returns))
1868	        # Ordering is important here. We expect the "mutable input" returns to come last.
1869	        returns = original_returns + returns_from_mutable_inputs
1870	
1871	        args_sig = self.arguments.signature(strip_default=strip_default)
1872	        # See Note [bernoulli.p schema]
1873	        if str(self.name) == "bernoulli.p":
1874	            args_sig = Arguments.parse(str(args_sig).replace("float p", "float p=0.5"))
1875	
1876	        return FunctionSchema(
1877	            name=OperatorName(
1878	                name=BaseOperatorName(
1879	                    base=base_name,
1880	                    inplace=False,
1881	                    dunder_method=self.name.name.dunder_method,
1882	                ),
1883	                overload_name="",  # stripped
1884	            ),
1885	            arguments=args_sig,
1886	            returns=returns,
1887	        )
1888	
1889	    def view_signature(self) -> FunctionSchema:
1890	        return self.signature(strip_view_copy_name=True)
1891	
1892	    def with_name(self, name: OperatorName) -> FunctionSchema:
1893	        return FunctionSchema(
1894	            name=name,
1895	            arguments=self.arguments,
1896	            returns=self.returns,
1897	        )
1898	
1899	    @property
1900	    def modifies_arguments(self) -> bool:
1901	        return self.kind() in [SchemaKind.inplace, SchemaKind.out, SchemaKind.mutable]
1902	
1903	    def has_symint(self) -> bool:
1904	        return self.arguments.has_symint_arg()
1905	
1906	    def __str__(self) -> str:
1907	        all_arguments_str = str(self.arguments)
1908	        if len(self.returns) == 1:
1909	            returns = str(self.returns[0])  # omit parentheses
1910	        else:
1911	            returns = "(" + ", ".join(map(str, self.returns)) + ")"
1912	        return f"{self.name}({all_arguments_str}) -> {returns}"
1913	
1914	
1915	# Here is the rest of the data model, described more briefly.
1916	
1917	
1918	# Simplified version for what actually shows up in built-ins.
1919	# Look at alias_info.h for expanded syntax.  If you need the structure,
1920	# you also need to make this structure recursive so it can be lined
1921	# up with the type components too.  For primitives this isn't really
1922	# necessary
1923	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1924	class Annotation:
1925	    # Typically only has one element.  Not actually a set so
1926	    # we can conveniently assume it is canonically ordered
1927	    alias_set: tuple[str, ...]
1928	    is_write: bool
1929	    alias_set_after: tuple[str, ...]
1930	
1931	    @staticmethod
1932	    def parse(ann: str) -> Annotation:
1933	        # TODO: implement a proper parser if this gets more ugly
1934	        # Regex Explanation:
1935	        # Example: "a! -> a|b"
1936	        # Group #1: alias before optional '|', required. Matches the first
1937	        #   character 'a' in the example
1938	        # Group #2: optional alias set after optional '|', matches empty string
1939	        #   in the example
1940	        # Group #3: optional "is write" flag, matches '!' in the example.
1941	        # Group #4: optional section containing arrow, matches " -> a|b" in the
1942	        #   example.
1943	        # Group #5: optional alias after set, supports wildcard, matches "a|b"
1944	        #   in the example.
1945	        # Group #6: optional sub-section of alias after set, matches "|b" in the
1946	        #   example.
1947	        m = re.match(r"^([a-z])(\|[a-z])*(!?)( -> (\*|[a-z](\|[a-z])*))?$", ann)
1948	
1949	        if m is None:
1950	            raise AssertionError(f"unrecognized alias annotation {ann}")
1951	        before_alias = m.group(1) + (m.group(2) if m.group(2) else "")
1952	        alias_set = tuple(before_alias.split("|"))
1953	        is_write = m.group(3) == "!"
1954	        if is_write and len(alias_set) > 1:
1955	            raise AssertionError(
1956	                f"alias set larger than 1 is not mutable, got {ann} instead."
1957	            )
1958	        after_set = tuple(m.group(5).split("|")) if m.group(5) else ()
1959	        if len(before_alias) > 1 and len(after_set) > 1:
1960	            raise AssertionError(
1961	                f"before alias set and after alias set cannot be larger than 1 at the same time, got {ann} instead."
1962	            )
1963	        r = Annotation(
1964	            alias_set=alias_set, is_write=is_write, alias_set_after=after_set
1965	        )
1966	        if str(r) != ann:
1967	            raise AssertionError(f"{r} != {ann}")
1968	        return r
1969	
1970	    def __str__(self) -> str:
1971	        alias_set = "|".join(self.alias_set)
1972	        if self.is_write:
1973	            alias_set = f"{alias_set}!"
1974	        alias_set_after = "|".join(self.alias_set_after)
1975	        if alias_set_after:
1976	            alias_set = f"{alias_set} -> {alias_set_after}"
1977	        return alias_set
1978	
1979	
1980	# The base class for the type system.  This is also loosely modeled
1981	# off of jit_type.h, but we've simplified the hierarchy to focus
1982	# in on the aspects of the type system that matter for code generation
1983	# (for example, there's no SingleElementType subclass anymore).
1984	# You never actually construct a Type; usually it's going to be one
1985	# of the subclasses.  If Python had ADTs this would be one!
1986	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1987	class Type:
1988	    @staticmethod
1989	    def parse(t: str) -> Type:
1990	        r = Type._parse(t)
1991	        if str(r) != t:
1992	            raise AssertionError(f"{r} != {t}")
1993	        return r
1994	
1995	    @staticmethod
1996	    def _parse(t: str) -> Type:
1997	        m = re.match(r"^(.+)\?$", t)
1998	        if m is not None:
1999	            return OptionalType(Type.parse(m.group(1)))
2000	        m = re.match(r"^(.+)\[([0-9]+)?\]$", t)
2001	        if m is not None:
2002	            size = int(m.group(2)) if m.group(2) is not None else None
2003	            return ListType(elem=Type.parse(m.group(1)), size=size)
2004	
2005	        # '__torch__.torch.classes.' is the prefix for custom class
2006	        m = re.match(r"^__torch__\.torch\.classes\.([a-zA-Z0-9_.]+)$", t)
2007	        if m is not None:
2008	            return CustomClassType(m.group(1))
2009	        try:
2010	            return BaseType(BaseTy[t])
2011	        except KeyError as e:
2012	            raise RuntimeError(f"unrecognized type {t}") from e
2013	
2014	    def __str__(self) -> str:
2015	        raise NotImplementedError
2016	
2017	    # WARNING: These concepts are not very well-defined.  For example,
2018	    # is "int?" nullable? How about "int?[]".  They are defined
2019	    # so we can conveniently generate legacy Declarations.yaml but
2020	    # really we should probably just remove these at some point
2021	
2022	    def is_base_ty_like(self, base_ty: BaseTy) -> bool:

2162	class Argument:
2163	    # NB: I didn't put kwarg_only as a boolean field here, unlike
2164	    # c10::Argument, so that printing works correctly
2165	
2166	    name: str
2167	    type: Type
2168	    default: str | None
2169	
2170	    # The semantics of the annotation field are a little strange.
2171	    #
2172	    # Alias annotations parametrize Tensors (since Tensors are the only things
2173	    # that can alias.)  This motivates why I write Tensor(a!)?  (and not, for
2174	    # example, Tensor?(a!)), because the (a!) describes aliasing on the tensor,
2175	    # which may be optional (i.e., the alias annotation should bind first to
2176	    # Tensor, before the optional postfix annotation).
2177	    #
2178	    # However, despite being a property of Tensor, we (and c10::Argument)
2179	    # store the annotation at the top level of the Argument, rather than
2180	    # inside the embedded Tensor type.  In the C++ version of this
2181	    # class, we then go through great lengths to mimic the type
2182	    # structure in the annotation structure so we can correlate
2183	    # annotations with types.
2184	    #
2185	    # Now, it turns out, in all applications in code generation, the
2186	    # structure of annotated types is very simple.  So we just hard
2187	    # code it here.  But if we ever do get anything more complex, this
2188	    # model will have to change!
2189	    annotation: Annotation | None
2190	
2191	    @property
2192	    def alias_info(self) -> Annotation | None:
2193	        return self.annotation
2194	
2195	    @staticmethod
2196	    def parse(arg: str) -> Argument:
2197	        name: str
2198	        default: str | None
2199	        if " " not in arg:
2200	            raise AssertionError(f"illegal argument '{arg}'")
2201	        if "=" in arg:
2202	            if arg.count("=") != 1:
2203	                raise AssertionError(f"illegal argument with default value: '{arg}'")
2204	            type_and_annot_and_name, default = arg.split("=")
2205	            type_and_annot, name = type_and_annot_and_name.rsplit(" ", 1)
2206	            name_and_default = f"{name}={default}"
2207	        else:
2208	            type_and_annot, name_and_default = arg.rsplit(" ", 1)
2209	            name = name_and_default
2210	            default = None
2211	        # TODO: deduplicate annotation matching with Return
2212	        match = re.match(r"Tensor\((.+)\)(.*)", type_and_annot)
2213	        annotation: Annotation | None
2214	        if match:
2215	            # If you update this, make sure the __str__ still works too
2216	            if match.group(2) not in ["", "?", "[]"]:
2217	                raise AssertionError(
2218	                    f"unrecognized alias analysis form with Tensor: {match.group(2)}"
2219	                )
2220	            type_s = "Tensor" + match.group(2)
2221	            annotation = Annotation.parse(match.group(1))
2222	        else:
2223	            type_s = type_and_annot
2224	            annotation = None
2225	        type = Type.parse(type_s)
2226	        r = Argument(
2227	            name=name,
2228	            type=type,
2229	            default=default,
2230	            annotation=annotation,
2231	        )
2232	        if str(r) != arg:
2233	            raise AssertionError(f"{str(r)} != {arg}")
2234	        return r
2235

2237	    def is_write(self) -> bool:
2238	        return self.annotation is not None and self.annotation.is_write

2244	                raise AssertionError(f"annotation on non-Tensor type: {type}")
2245	            type = type.replace("Tensor", f"Tensor({self.annotation})")
2246	        if self.name is None:
2247	            return type
2248	        else:
2249	            mb_default = ""
2250	            if self.default:
2251	                mb_default = f"={self.default}"
2252	            return f"{type} {self.name}{mb_default}"

2321	@dataclass(frozen=True)
2322	class TensorOptionsArguments:
